templates.label: First live API call (PowerShell)

sections.api_30_seconds: Program -> HTTP POST + JSON + API key -> xAI -> JSON response -> credits deducted.
product_picker: Live phone voice -> Voice Agents realtime
guardrails: Learn with short text calls first
Do not leave realtime voice running unattended

templates.label: Module 1 checklist
items: I can explain API in one sentence: program sends structured request, gets structured response
I know browser Grok vs API is same models, different access path
I can name one thing I did (voice clone, app) and which API category it used

templates.label: Module 2 checklist
items: I can identify credits, tokens, and requests on my console
I know all four products draw from the same $50 pool
I understand the API key is a billing password for programs

templates.label: Module 3 checklist
items: I can name URL, headers, body as the three request parts
I understand model, system, user roles in JSON
I know input_tokens and output_tokens drive cost
I ran the PowerShell example OR know exactly what success looks like

templates.label: Module 4 checklist
items: I know text vs voice vs video cost order of magnitude
I used the calculator for one scenario I care about
I have a decision on auto top-up (on/off) with a reason

templates.label: Module 5 checklist
items: I understand tool calling = model triggers my code
I can distinguish TTS vs realtime voice agents
I picked which API matches my next actual project
